events.title: EarlyON Family Resource Program
events.description: EarlyON, where families come together to learn, play, and connect. EarlyON Child and Family programs offer free, inclusive and welcoming programs for parents, caregivers and young children from birth to six years old. These high-quality programs are designed to cater to the diverse needs of families and children.  This is a drop in program. No registration required.  For more information please contact the Port Union Library: 416-396-8885.

events.title: EarlyON Baby Lap Time
events.description: Join us for weekly discussions on parenting and infant care. Meet other parents, learn new songs, rhymes and finger plays, and foster bonding with your baby! Ages 0 to 11 months.

events.title: Early ON Play and Learn
events.description: Parents, caregivers and their children socialize with others in an interactive learning environment. Children learn, explore, communicate and understand new concepts, expanding their knowledge through play-based and guided activities. This includes songs, rhymes and reading stories, along with gross and fine motor activities. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Mothercraft Drop In by EarlyON
events.description: Join us for Mothercraft Drop-In by EarlyON, for children aged 0-6 and their caregivers, where participants will engage with early-years professionals, and connect with others in the community. Through a variety of fun activities, books, songs, and play, this program aims to help strengthen adult-child relationships, support parent education, and foster healthy child development.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. If your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services at accessibleservices@tpl.ca or phone 416-393-7099. Please contact us at least three weeks in advance.

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

Children under 12 need to be supervised.

Participants are also welcome to bring their own items from home to decorate. For children and their families. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: STEAM Lab
events.description: Join us for our weekly STEAM Lab (Ages 6-12) , where kids can explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math through fun, hands-on activities. From building simple robots and experimenting with Snap Circuits to creative design challenges and more, there’s always something new to discover. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

This camp will take place over 5 days (July 6-10); registration is for all dates listed. events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop
events.description: Chef Yumiko leads a hands-on workshop to make the popular Asian beverage, Brown Sugar Oat Milk with Boba. Participants will learn to prepare and use the classics ingredients including oat milk and tapioca pearls to make this visually pleasing and sweet drink. Chef Yumiko will also provide a brief history of Boba and why it's so popular globally.

events.title: Storytelling and Afrikan Drumming
events.description: Storyteller and professinal percussionist, Kwanza Msingwana, performs folktales and traditional stories from Afrika and around the world. This is a drop-in program. No registration is required.

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around Taylor Memorial
events.description: Taylor Memorial Branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. events.title: Anne of the Library-on-the-Hill
events.description: 'Growing up in the shadow of the Great War, Anne finds comfort in her neighbourhood library, where she loses herself in books, often imagining herself as part of the story. She particularly loves the books of LM Montgomery — and her imagination really takes off when she learns her beloved author is in town.' "Anne of the Library-On-The-Hill" (Plumleaf Press, August 2024), presented by the book's author Catherine Little, celebrates iconic Canadian author, Lucy Maud Montgomery, and her connection

events.title: Yoga Storytime for kids
events.description: Appleseed Yoga presents an interactive, movement-based program where stories come alive with our bodies! Class begins and ends with song and breathwork and is filled with pages of creativity and activity. No experience, mats, or materials required. Ages 4-8. Registration required

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around  the Branch Tour
events.description: The North York Central Library and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood walk. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Please note, the tour will start and end at the North York Central Library, Room 1, and will be guided by the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff

events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: All Ages Karaoke and Board Game Night
events.description: Come sing your favourite tunes on our stage in a fun, encouraging environment. Request any song and we'll try to find a karaoke video and play it for you! If singing is not your thing, a few board games will be on hand if you want to try out Catan, Anomia, Ticket to Ride, or test your vocabulary with Scrabble! You are also welcome to bring your own and invite others to play with you. Children must be accompanied by an adult.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: All skill levels are welcome! Chess sets are provided.

events.title: POV: You Found a Good Book
events.description: Not your average book club. Think less assigned reading, more good vibes, real talk, and stories that actually hit. No pressure, no quizzes—just snacks, chill convos, and books you might actually want to finish. Come hang out, meet people your age, and help pick what we read next. You might even find a book you don’t hate.  Drop in. Bring a friend. Or just bring yourself—we saved you a spot. The Hate U Give

events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters.
